name: First and last occurrence
statement: Given an array of integers nums sorted in non-decreasing order, find the starting and ending position of a given target value. If the target is not found in the array, return [-1, -1].
constraints:
•   0 <= nums.length <= 105
  •   -109 <= nums[i] <= 109
  •   nums is a non-decreasing array.
  •   -109 <= target <= 109

examples.explanation: The target is 8, and it appears in the array at indices 3 and 4, so the output is [3,4]

examples.output: [-1, -1] Expalantion: The target is 6, which is not present in the array. Therefore, the output is [-1, -1].

testcases.name: Case 2
testcases.expected: [-1, -1] Expalantion: The target is 6, which is not present in the array. Therefore, the output is [-1, -1].
